name: sap-tools
description: All-in-one toolbox for SAP BTP development workflows

sapCap.currentScope.markdownDescription: Currently active SAP BTP scope managed by SAP Tools.

sapTools.sharedCapDebugConfig.markdownDescription: Shared remote-source hints for artifact export. SAP Tools reads this first and falls back to `cdsDebug.sharedCapDebugConfig` (the CDS Debug extension), so a single configuration serves both extensions.

remoteRoot.markdownDescription: Remote source root inside the CF app container used to locate exported artifacts such as `pnpm-lock.yaml`. Use a fixed path like `/home/vcap/app`, or a regex with `regex:<pattern>` or `/pattern/flags`; regex values are resolved per CF app through `cf ssh` (matched against the folders that contain a `package.json`). When unset or unmatched, SAP Tools falls back to the standard `/home/vcap/app` location.

sapTools.appFolderMappings.markdownDescription: Explicit Cloud Foundry app name → local folder name mappings for the Apps tab service-folder mapping. Use this when a CF app's name differs too much from its local source folder for the automatic `-`↔`_` normalization to find it. `folderName` is a folder *basename* searched recursively (depth ≤ 6) for a folder with that name containing a `package.json`; it has the highest matching priority. SAP Tools merges these with the CDS Debug extension's `cdsDebug.appFolderMappings` (own entries win on conflicting app names), so a single configuration serves both extensions.

folderName.markdownDescription: Local source folder name (basename) to resolve this app to.

sapTools.localPackages.namePatterns.markdownDescription: Comma-separated patterns identifying which repos under the Apps-tab root folder are locally-developed npm packages (as opposed to service repos). Each pattern is tested against the `name` in a folder's `package.json`; a pattern that is not valid regex is matched literally, so a plain scope prefix like `@example/` works. Detection keys off the package *name*, not the presence of a `build` script (so dependency-only packages such as `@example/core` are still found). Leave empty to disable local-package scanning.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Before publishing, temporarily set a unique prerelease version (`<base>-local.<timestamp>`) so the local registry accepts the republish, then restore the original version in `package.json`.
Publish the version as-is. Republishing the same version will fail unless you bump it yourself.
sapTools.localPackages.versionBumpStrate.markdownDescription: How to make a package version unique for each publish to the local registry. Verdaccio rejects republishing an existing version; `prerelease-timestamp` works around this without editing your git history (the original version is restored after publish).

sapTools.localPackages.installInServiceA.markdownDescription: After building and publishing the packages a service needs, run `npm install` in that service folder (with the configured scopes pointed at the local registry) to pull the freshly published versions.

sapTools.localRegistry.port.markdownDescription: Port the self-hosted local Verdaccio registry listens on (`http://localhost:<port>`).

sapTools.localRegistry.scopes.markdownDescription: Comma-separated npm scopes (e.g. `@example`) routed to the local registry when publishing and installing. When empty, SAP Tools derives the scopes from `sapTools.localPackages.namePatterns`.

sapTools.localRegistry.defaultTag.markdownDescription: Fallback npm dist-tag to publish local packages under. SAP Tools first tries to reuse the tag a service requests in its `package.json` (e.g. `"@example/core": "staging"`); this value is used when that dependency value is a semver range rather than a plain tag.

sapTools.localRegistry.autoStart.markdownDescription: Start the local Verdaccio registry automatically when SAP Tools activates (only takes effect once it has been installed on first use).

scripts.validate:root: npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run cspell && npm run test:unit
scripts.validate: npm run validate:root && npm run validate:e2e
